sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: ξανθοῖσι βοστρύχοισιν εὐοσμῶν κόμην ,
sections.translation: fragrant in hair with golden curls , having in his eyes the wine-dark graces of Aphrodite . He is with the young girls day and night , alluring them with joyful mysteries . If I catch him within this house ,

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: παύσω κτυποῦντα θύρσον ἀνασείοντά τε
sections.translation: I will stop him from making a noise with the thyrsos and shaking his hair , by cutting his head off . That one claims that Dionysus is a god , claims that he was once stitched into the thigh of Zeus — Dionysus , who was burnt up with his mother by the flame of lightning ,

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: σὺν μητρί , Δίους ὅτι γάμους ἐψεύσατο .
sections.translation: because she had falsely claimed a marriage with Zeus . Is this not worthy of a terrible death by hanging , for a stranger to insult me with these insults , whoever he is ? But here is another wonder — I see Teiresias the soothsayer in dappled fawn-skins

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: πατέρα τε μητρὸς τῆς ἐμῆσ — πολὺν γέλων —
sections.translation: and my mother ’ s father — a great absurdity — raging about with a thyrsos . I shrink , father , from seeing your old age devoid of sense . Won ’ t you cast away the ivy ? Grandfather , will you not free your hand of the thyrsos ?

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: σὺ ταῦτʼ ἔπεισας , Τειρεσία · τόνδʼ αὖ θέλεις
sections.translation: You persuaded him to this , Teiresias . Do you wish , by introducing another new god to men , to examine birds and receive rewards for sacrifices ? If your gray old age did not defend you , you would sit in chains in the midst of the Bacchae ,

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: τελετὰς πονηρὰς εἰσάγων · γυναιξὶ γὰρ
sections.translation: for introducing wicked rites . For where women have the delight of the grape-cluster at a feast , I say that none of their rites is healthy any longer .

sections.label: Section 263
sections.original: τῆς δυσσεβείας . ὦ ξένʼ , οὐκ αἰδῇ θεοὺς
sections.translation: Oh , what impiety ! O stranger , do you not reverence the gods and Kadmos who sowed the earth-born crop ?

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: Ἐχίονος δʼ ὢν παῖς καταισχύνεις γένος ;
sections.translation: Do you , the child of Echion , bring shame to your race ?

sections.label: Section 266
sections.original: ὅταν λάβῃ τις τῶν λόγων ἀνὴρ σοφὸς
sections.translation: Whenever a wise man takes a good occasion for his speech , it is not a great task to speak well . You have a rapid tongue as though you were sensible , but there is no sense in your words .

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: θράσει δὲ δυνατὸς καὶ λέγειν οἷός τʼ ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: A man powerful in his boldness , one capable of speaking well , becomes a bad citizen in his lack of sense . This new god , whom you ridicule , I am unable to express how great he will be throughout Hellas . For two things , young man ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: τὰ πρῶτʼ ἐν ἀνθρώποισι · Δημήτηρ θεά —
sections.translation: are first among men : the goddess Demeter — she is the earth , but call her whatever name you wish ; she nourishes mortals with dry food ; but he who came afterwards , the offspring of Semele , discovered a match to it , the liquid drink of the grape , and introduced it

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: θνητοῖς , ὃ παύει τοὺς ταλαιπώρους βροτοὺς
sections.translation: to mortals . It releases wretched mortals from grief , whenever they are filled with the stream of the vine , and gives them sleep , a means of forgetting their daily troubles , nor is there another cure for hardships . He who is a god is poured out in offerings to the gods ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: ὥστε διὰ τοῦτον τἀγάθʼ ἀνθρώπους ἔχειν .
sections.translation: so that by his means men may have good things . And do you laugh at him , because he was sewn up in Zeus ’ thigh ? I will teach you that this is well : when Zeus snatched him out of the lighting-flame , and led the child as a god to Olympus ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: Ἥρα νιν ἤθελʼ ἐκβαλεῖν ἀπʼ οὐρανοῦ ·
sections.translation: Hera wished to banish him from the sky , but Zeus , as a god , had a counter-contrivance . Having broken a part of the air which surrounds the earth , he gave this to Hera as a pledge protecting the real [*] Dionysus from her hostility . But in time ,

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: βροτοὶ ῥαφῆναί φασιν ἐν μηρῷ Διός ,
sections.translation: mortals say that he was nourished in the thigh of Zeus , changing the word , because a god he had served as a hostage for the goddess Hera , and composing the story . [*] 298 But this god is a prophet — for Bacchic revelry and madness have in them much prophetic skill .

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ὅταν γὰρ ὁ θεὸς ἐς τὸ σῶμʼ ἔλθῃ πολύς ,
sections.translation: For whenever the god enters a body in full force , he makes the frantic to foretell the future . He also possesses a share of Ares ’ nature . For terror sometimes flutters an army under arms and in its ranks before it even touches a spear ;

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: μανία δὲ καὶ τοῦτʼ ἐστὶ Διονύσου πάρα .
sections.translation: and this too is a frenzy from Dionysus . You will see him also on the rocks of Delphi , bounding with torches through the highland of two peaks , leaping and shaking the Bacchic branch , mighty throughout Hellas . But believe me , Pentheus ;

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: μὴ τὸ κράτος αὔχει δύναμιν ἀνθρώποις ἔχειν ,
sections.translation: do not boast that sovereignty has power among men , nor , even if you think so , and your mind is diseased , believe that you are being at all wise . Receive the god into your land , pour libations to him , celebrate the Bacchic rites , and garland your head . Dionysus will not compel women

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: γυναῖκας ἐς τὴν Κύπριν , ἀλλʼ ἐν τῇ φύσει
sections.translation: to be modest in regard to Aphrodite , but in nature modesty dwells always you must look for that . For she who is modest will not be corrupted in Bacchic revelry . Do you see ? You rejoice whenever many people are at your gates ,

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: πολλοί , τὸ Πενθέως δʼ ὄνομα μεγαλύνῃ πόλις ·
sections.translation: and the city extols the name of Pentheus . He too , I think , delights in being honored . Kadmos , whom you mock , and I will crown our heads with ivy and dance , a gray yoke-team but still we must dance ;

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: κοὐ θεομαχήσω σῶν λόγων πεισθεὶς ὕπο .
sections.translation: and I will not be persuaded by your words to fight against the god . For you are mad in a most grievous way , and you will not be cured by drugs , nor are you sick without them .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: ὦ πρέσβυ , Φοῖβόν τʼ οὐ καταισχύνεις λόγοις ,
sections.translation: Old man , you do not shame Phoebus with your words , and honoring Dionysus , a great god , you are prudent .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , καλῶς σοι Τειρεσίας παρῄνεσεν .
sections.translation: My child , Teiresias has advised you well . Dwell with us , not apart from the laws . For now you flit about and have thoughts without thinking . Even if , as you say , he is not a god , call him one ; and tell a glorious falsehood ,

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: ὡς ἔστι , Σεμέλη θʼ ἵνα δοκῇ θεὸν τεκεῖν ,
sections.translation: so that Semele might seem to have borne a god , and honor might come to all our race . You see the wretched fate of Actaeon , who was torn apart in the meadows by the blood-thirsty hounds he had raised ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: Ἀρτέμιδος εἶναι κομπάσαντʼ , ἐν ὀργάσιν .
sections.translation: having boasted that he was superior in the hunt to Artemis . May you not suffer this . Come , let me crown your head with ivy ; honor the god along with us .

sections.label: Section 343
sections.original: οὐ μὴ προσοίσεις χεῖρα , βακχεύσεις δʼ ἰών ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t lay a hand on me ! Go off and hold your revels , but don ’ t wipe your foolishness off on me . I will seek the punishment of this

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: τῆς σῆς δʼ ἀνοίας τόνδε τὸν διδάσκαλον
sections.translation: teacher of your folly . Let someone go quickly to the seat where he watches the flights of birds , upset and overturn it with levers , turning everything upside down ;

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: καὶ στέμματʼ ἀνέμοις καὶ θυέλλαισιν μέθες .
sections.translation: and release his garlands to the winds and storms . In this way I will especially wound him . And some of you hunt throughout the city for this effeminate stranger , who introduces a new disease to women and pollutes our beds .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: κἄνπερ λάβητε , δέσμιον πορεύσατε
sections.translation: If you catch him , bring him here bound , so that he might suffer as punishment a death by stoning , having seen a bitter Bacchic revelry in Thebes .

sections.label: Section 358
sections.original: ὦ σχέτλιʼ , ὡς οὐκ οἶσθα ποῦ ποτʼ εἶ λόγων .
sections.translation: O wretched man , how little you know what you are saying ! You are mad now , and even before you were out of your wits .

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: στείχωμεν ἡμεῖς , Κάδμε , κἀξαιτώμεθα
sections.translation: Let us go , Kadmos , and entreat the god , on behalf of him , though he is savage , and on behalf of the city , to do no ill . But follow me with the ivy-clad staff , and try to support my body , and I will try to support yours ;

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: γέροντε δʼ αἰσχρὸν δύο πεσεῖν · ἴτω δʼ ὅμως ,
sections.translation: it would be shameful for two old men to fall down . But let that pass , for we must serve Bacchus , the son of Zeus . Beware lest Pentheus bring trouble to your house , Kadmos ; I do not speak in prophecy , but judging from the state of things ; for a foolish man speaks foolishness .

sections.label: Section 1.370
sections.original: Ὁσία πότνα θεῶν ,
sections.translation: Holiness , queen of the gods , Holiness , who bear your golden wings along the earth , do you hear these words from Pentheus ? Do you hear his unholy

sections.label: Section 1.375
sections.original: ὕβριν ἐς τὸν Βρόμιον , τὸν
sections.translation: insolence against Bromius , the child of Semele , the first deity of the gods at the banquets where guests wear beautiful garlands ? He holds this office , to join in dances ,

sections.label: Section 1.380
sections.original: μετά τʼ αὐλοῦ γελάσαι
sections.translation: to laugh with the flute , and to bring an end to cares , whenever the delight of the grape comes at the feasts of the gods , and in ivy-bearing banquets

sections.label: Section 1.385
sections.original: δράσι κρατὴρ ὕπνον ἀμ-
sections.translation: the goblet sheds sleep over men .

sections.label: Section 1.386
sections.original: ἀχαλίνων στομάτων
sections.translation: Misfortune is the result of unbridled mouths and lawless folly ; but the life of quiet

sections.label: Section 1.390
sections.original: βίοτος καὶ τὸ φρονεῖν
sections.translation: and wisdom remain unshaken and hold houses together . Though they dwell far off in the heavens the gods see the deeds of mortals .

sections.label: Section 1.395
sections.original: τὸ σοφὸν δʼ οὐ σοφία
sections.translation: But cleverness is not wisdom , nor is thinking on things unfit for mortals . Life is short , and on this account the one who pursues great things does not achieve that which is present . In my opinion ,

sections.label: Section 1.400
sections.original: νομένων οἵδε τρόποι καὶ
sections.translation: these are the ways of mad and ill-advised men .

sections.label: Section 2.402
sections.original: ἱκοίμαν ποτὶ Κύπρον ,
sections.translation: Would that I could go to Cyprus , the island of Aphrodite , where the Loves , who soothe

sections.label: Section 2.405
sections.original: ται θνατοῖσιν Ἔρωτες ,
sections.translation: mortals ’ hearts , dwell , and to Paphos , fertilized without rain by the streams of a foreign river flowing with a hundred mouths . Lead me there , Bromius , Bromius , god of joy who leads the Bacchae ,

sections.label: Section 2.410
sections.original: Πιερία μούσειος ἕδρα ,
sections.translation: to Pieria , beautiful seat of the Muses , the holy slope of Olympus . There are the Graces , there is Desire ; there it is

sections.label: Section 2.415
sections.original: ἐκεῖ δὲ Πόθος · ἐκεῖ δὲ βάκ-
sections.translation: lawful for the Bacchae to celebrate their rites .

sections.label: Section 2.417
sections.original: ὁ δαίμων ὁ Διὸς παῖς
sections.translation: The god , the son of Zeus , delights in banquets , and loves Peace , giver of riches ,

sections.label: Section 2.420
sections.original: ρήναν , κουροτρόφον θεάν .
sections.translation: goddess who nourishes youths . To the blessed and to the less fortunate , he gives an equal pleasure from wine that banishes grief . He hates the one who does not care about this :

sections.label: Section 2.425
sections.original: κατὰ φάος νύκτας τε φίλας
sections.translation: to lead a happy life by day and friendly [*] night and to keep his wise mind and intellect away from over-curious men .

sections.label: Section 2.430
sections.original: τὸ πλῆθος ὅ τι
sections.translation: What the common people think and adopt , that would I accept .

sections.label: Section 434
sections.original: Πενθεῦ , πάρεσμεν τήνδʼ ἄγραν ἠγρευκότες
sections.translation: Pentheus , we are here , having caught this prey

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ἐφʼ ἣν ἔπεμψας , οὐδʼ ἄκρανθʼ ὡρμήσαμεν .
sections.translation: for which you sent us , nor have we set out in vain . This beast was docile in our hands and did not withdraw in flight , but yielded not unwillingly . He did not turn pale or change the wine-dark complexion of his cheek , but laughed and allowed us to bind him and lead him away .

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ἔμενέ τε , τοὐμὸν εὐτρεπὲς ποιούμενος .
sections.translation: He remained still , making my work easy , and I in shame said : Stranger , I do not lead you away willingly , but by order of Pentheus , who sent me . And the Bacchae whom you shut up , whom you carried off and bound in the chains of the public prison ,

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: φροῦδαί γʼ ἐκεῖναι λελυμέναι πρὸς ὀργάδας
sections.translation: are set loose and gone , and are gamboling in the meadows , invoking Bromius as their god . Of their own accord , the chains were loosed from their feet and keys opened the doors without human hand . This man has come to Thebes

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: ἐς τάσδε Θήβας . σοὶ δὲ τἄλλα χρὴ μέλειν .
sections.translation: full of many wonders . You must take care of the rest .

sections.label: Section 451
sections.original: μέθεσθε χειρῶν τοῦδʼ · ἐν ἄρκυσιν γὰρ ὢν
sections.translation: Release his hands , for caught in the nets he is not so swift as to escape me . But your body is not ill-formed , stranger , for women ’ s purposes , for which reason you have come to Thebes .

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: πλόκαμός τε γάρ σου ταναός , οὐ πάλης ὕπο ,
sections.translation: For your hair is long , not through wrestling , scattered over your cheeks , full of desire ; and you have a white skin from careful preparation , hunting after Aphrodite by your beauty not exposed to strokes of the sun , but beneath the shade .

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν οὖν μοι λέξον ὅστις εἶ γένος .
sections.translation: First then tell me who your family is .

sections.label: Section 461
sections.original: οὐ κόμπος οὐδείς · ῥᾴδιον δʼ εἰπεῖν τόδε .
sections.translation: I can tell you this easily , without boasting . I suppose you are familiar with flowery Tmolus .

sections.label: Section 463
sections.original: οἶδʼ , ὃς τὸ Σάρδεων ἄστυ περιβάλλει κύκλῳ .
sections.translation: I know of it ; it surrounds the city of Sardis .

sections.label: Section 464
sections.original: ἐντεῦθέν εἰμι , Λυδία δέ μοι πατρίς .
sections.translation: I am from there , and Lydia is my fatherland .

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: πόθεν δὲ τελετὰς τάσδʼ ἄγεις ἐς Ἑλλάδα ;
sections.translation: Why do you bring these rites to Hellas ?
